entry.title: Re: Initial Release of the Lite Viewer for SAP Analytics Cloud Stories
entry.content:
I admit to Jonshelly,
we have also opened a new ticket.
We do have the behavior for some OVM stories, that for some widget the data are not shown anymore. For this we do the same an use the URL parameter mode=embed, this works fine.
Nevertheless, we expect a solution like, the possibilit to switch enable / disable checkbox in story properties to turn this on and off.
Otherwise, the LIte-Viewer shouldn't bring up any problems, lik ea mentioned.

Best regards
Cengiz

entry.title: Re: Display DOCX/XLSX in Archivelink document viewer from...
entry.content:
Hello,

I've seen your answers.

The viewer inside SAPGUI you use today is possible thanks to the OLE integration between the SAPGUI and the Office applications on your workstation (desktop or laptop).

With the Web, this is not possible anymore, because of the use of a web browser instead of the SAPGUI. This is the same problem with all SAP UIs as from the moment you use the web.

In a web browser, there is no way to have the exact same behaviour that you have today with the SAPGUI. However there are some possibilities to have simllar behaviours. For example if your attachments are stored in SharePoint, then they can be opened directly in the corresponding Office online application (word, excel, powerpoint, pdf, ...). Behaviour is as follows: You are in SAP WebGui, you click on the attachment -> a new window opens and displays the attachment directly in the office online application.

Note that this behaviour requires a specific ABAP addon to store the attachments in SharePoint and set the correct links in the SAP screens. Let me know if this could be of interest for you.

entry.title: Re: Web IDE Could not deploy the builder MTA.
entry.content:
@michael_devine thx for your answer

 

I guess you can try to set the domain to be rammi-laptop-i7.sap.com

 

I try various domains:

  • hana-express.develop
  • rammi-laptop-i7.hana-express.develop

I try with update the /etc/hostname with the domains above or just with the origin entry.

Always the same, but with side effects like: Chrome/Chromium does not work anymore. After reset the /etc/hostname the side effects are gone.

 

What is the full error text when you deploy the builder?

I've a extended thread about with the whole logs. (maybe the forum admin could united both)

Sebastian

I have created lable using smartforms & printing this with the help of DYMO printer.

Currently this text is print in horizontally, We need in Vertical or landscape mode.

Please help & tell us any suggestion.

PFA.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Anjali Gore.

entry.title: Re: 供應商發票 Create API 只能帶入一組<glob:SupplierInvoiceERPCreate...
entry.content: 只看到ODATA API下面有batch request，可以考虑使用ODATA. 需要说明的是，如果是想省钱，即使用batch也同样会收费，因为是按照单据条目数收费，而不是API call的次数收费的。

entry.title: Re: F4 help for custom application job

Hi Bharath,

value help is not yet supported for customer coding. It will be available with one of the next releases.

Kind regards

Michael Szardenings

Product Owner Application Jobs

entry.title: Plant Maintenance & Customer Service - upgrade or conversion from ECC EHP6 to S4 HANA 2023

I need your quick help, please.

We are on an upgrade/conversion project, from ECC EHP6 to S4 HANA 2023.

I need help for - what would be Plant Maintenance & Customer Service activities that needs to be done post upgrade, after upgrade... I mean, any config, etc... post upgrade ?

I need quick help, please.

Have a Great Day...

entry.title: Re: Resource scheduling app- Order operation is confirmed...
entry.content:
Greetings,

perhaps this is related to the new phase and sub-phase model and has nothing to do with status of the operation on order. Check your settings following the SPRO path if it might indicate your scenario. Also please post Fiori APP ID of the scheduling app you are using. Can you also show a screen shot of IW33 of operations of your order - with status visible.

SPRO ->Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Fiori Apps for Maintenance Processing -> General Settings -> Define Subphases and Map to Overall Statuses

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 – Business Use Cases
entry.content:
Once SAP AI Core services are integrated into MDK (See Part 1), there will be numerous possibilities for MDK developers to implement AI-related use cases for end users. Let me introduce one such use case: generating structured data from multimodal inputs such as text, voice, and image using Generative AI. This just serves as a small step into a new universe of AI capabilities for MDK developers.

In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) to develop mobile applications with AI capabilities.

  • Part 1: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup
  • Part 2: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Business Use Cases (Current Blog)
  • Part 3: SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services - Measurement Readings (Coming Soon)

 

SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) integration with SAP AI Core services – Business Use Cases

Enable Multimodality AI in MDK: Incorporating multimodal AI in mobile development with MDK allows for integration of various input methods, such as free text input, photo capture, and audio and video recording. Following are a few potential scenarios:

  • Measurement readings: Capture and record correct measurements.
  • Anomaly detection:  Detect and report anomalies/irregularities in real time.
  • Maintenance guidelines generation: Automatically generate maintenance guidelines based on gathered data.
  • Work order and operation recording: Efficiently record and track work orders and operation.

To enable multimodality AI in app development using MDK, we can incorporate various input methods such as free text input, photo capture, audio and video recording. This allows users to interact with the app in a more natural and versatile manner. For instance, technicians can take measurement readings directly within the app, which can then be analyzed for anomaly detection. This helps in identifying potential issues before they escalate. Additionally, the app can generate maintenance guidelines based on the data collected, ensuring users have access to up-to-date and relevant information. Furthermore, the app can facilitate the recording of work orders and operations, streamlining the workflow and ensuring that all activities are documented accurately. By  enabling these AI capabilities, we can create a comprehensive and efficient solution that leverages the power of multimodality AI to enhance app functionality and user experience.

 

Use Case – Meter Reading and Equipment Maintenance

1. Use camera to take meter reading.

2. AI to detect anomaly with camera and predict maintenance.

3. AI to generate service operation to-do list

 

Use Case - Record Work Order and Operation

1. Use voice or free text to input plain text.

2. Save the input as unstructured data

3. Conduct a similarity search in the vector database to construct a prompt.

 

4. Send the prompt to a foundational LLM model to generate structured information.

 

5. Populate the generated information into a form and confirm the data transaction.

 

The architectural diagram and workflow of these use cases are shown below.

 

Step-by-step flow in the above workflow:

Step 1: begins with the MDK developer to enable SAP AI core services, as described in the previous blog

Step 2 and 3: involve using a Retrieval Argument Generation (RAG) embedding model, such as text-embedding-ada-002, which is optional for general use, to embed and index domain-specific knowledge of your use case. This includes input text chunking, creation of vector embeddings, and storing and deletion of the vector embeddings in the Embedding Storage of the SAP HANA Cloud vector engine. These steps are optional; if the publicly available foundational LLM already meets the requirements of your use cases, you can skip the RAG process.  I will publish a separated blog on the details of RAG.

Next, we will explain how everything works together from the end user's perspective.

Step 4:  the end user will interact with the MDK client using various multimodal inputs such as text, images, audio, and video. This interaction can include speaking to the mobile device, taking pictures, and recording audio or video. The MDK client will then use these inputs to construct a prompt.

Step 5: If your use case requires RAG, it will proceed to retrieve knowledge from the HANA Vector Database.

Step 6: The retrieved knowledge will be combined with the end user's input to form a prompt and sent to the Multimodal AI. The Multimodal AI is a deployment of the SAP AI Core service you set up in the previous blog. Keep in mind that the prompt here can also be considered unstructured data such as a picture just taken, a voice recording, or text just keyed in.

Step 7: The Multimodal AI will generate structured data according to the specifications in your prompt. For our use cases, this will be a JSON file containing key-value pairs, which are transformed from your unstructured inputs such as images or audio. The structured data output will be populated into the MDK client and bound to the UI controls accordingly. This allows the user to interact with the UI controls to check the meter readings, detect any anomalies, view a list of recommended To-Do items from the AI, and complete the work order subsequently.

Step 8: Once the user confirms all this information via the UI controls, the transaction data will be sent to the backend.

So far, we have explained our use cases step by step. In the upcoming blogs, we will delve into the MDK project for these use cases and explain the implementation with code examples.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Background Processing Framework
entry.content:
Hi @MattDion,

no, this is not possible: The background session is tied to the user who triggered it. 

Regards,
Andreas

entry.title: Re: No item category assigned to account
entry.content: THanks for the hints provided. I had a similar issue. there was an overlap in the account provided in the GL settings in document splitting. When I removed the overlap, it worked. Thanks a lot....

entry.title: Re: Clean Code: Writing maintainable, readable and testable code
entry.content:
Regarding the question: Why did you choose to move away from the open-source approach for Clean UI5? 

It was the original intention when we started the style guide repository for ABAP to do the same for other languages / frameworks. So far we could not find motivated colleagues who can continuously commit sufficient amount of time to create and maintain such a guide for SAPUI5 or CAP in an Open Source manner.

entry.title: SAP Business Network Commerce - Price
entry.content: How many Transactions are in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud inculded to work with Digital Supplier Network (BNC)?

entry.title: Re: Determine class structure / components (local class, test classes, ...)
entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi of which table?

entry.title: Re: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Hello

it is good to know that we can now generate a "basic SDS". But what about the "Cover Sheet" part? Will this come soon in a Cloud Version?

Regarding the topic 

" Can you suggest what alternative options there are to generate a safety data sheet for the time being on 2023? Can the compliance/safety information maintained in S/4 Product compliance be sent to an external system to generate the form? I may have missed this in your "demo 2" blog, I also cannot see any concrete information on sending the maintained data to an external form creation system..."

The current version of Product Compliance (even the "cloud version) does not have a good shapre to maintain data for  the 16 sections of a SDS (and i ignore here the Annex EU part).

But you can still use EHS Classic to generate a SDS and upload it to Product Compliance to sent it there to the customer

I propose: check the current Roadmap of "Product Compliance" and check the corresponding OSS note in which SAP is tryiing to describe the goal of the new solution (and more specific: the timeline of a functionality to come).

If you would like to start soon with Product Compliance: only the Cloud version is currently an option (from my point of view).

C.B

@HarshC this is not correct, it would be ABC~MaterialCreate with id interfaceDetermination_XYZ

Alex

Sharan

entry.title: Re: Determine class structure / components (local class,...
entry.content:
My solution looks at the moment kind of like this:

" 1. Select necessary records of TADIR table
SELECT
  FIELDS *
  FROM tadir ...

" 2. Get name of include for test classes
DATA(test_classes) = cl_oo_classname_service=>get_ccau_name( ).

" 3. Read source code of include to check if test classes exist or not
READ REPORT test_classes INTO source_code_test_classes.

" 4. Check if test classes exist

Now I am wondering if there is a simpler way to check if test classes exist in the include instead of parsing its whole source code...

Maybe there is another database table aswell where a marker for each local test class is stored?

Maybe anything like the ZCL_....=CMETH=001 for class methods also for test classes exists ZCL_...=CCUA001?

@helmut_skolaut3 I agree, right now JMS monitoring is quite hard, however we plan to improve this by offering filter options in future

Alex

entry.title: Re: Loading inception-to-date balances
entry.content: Data would look like this:

entry.title: Re: how to set a constant attribute to strans xml transformation?
entry.content:
I couldn't get a closing tag even withount xmlns tag :
Mine xml, i want to get : <issuance sender="700069297"></issuance>
mine trans : 

 

 

 

<?sap.transform simple?>
<tt:transform xmlns:tt="http://www.sap.com/transformation-templates" xmlns:ddic="http://www.sap.com/abapxml/types/dictionary" xmlns:def="http://www.sap.com/abapxml/types/defined">
  <tt:root name="ROOT" type="ddic:ZVA_ESCHF_GET"/>
  <tt:template>
    <issuance>

       <tt:attribute name="sender" value-ref="ROOT.SENDER"/>
    </issuance>
  </tt:template>
</tt:transform>

entry.title: Re: IBAN number maintained in Vendor Master - Table req
entry.content: You can use table BUT0BK.

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset Master data and capitalization for the de...
entry.content: You can make a debit of the value already depreciated as a fixed asset and, as a counterpart, a credit of the same value as accumulated depreciation.

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content: Hi @pboulicaut , Thank you for feedback. It is exposed in displayName of User Odata v2 API. You can refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/d599f15995d348a1b45ba5603e2aba9b/e13d5de2c3844adca3d1161f78fc638c.html with more details. General Display Name is person based, one employee can have only one general display name/ it doesn't matter whether the employee has multiple assignment or not.

entry.title: Re: Loading inception-to-date balances
entry.content:
Data would look like this...

period doc typesubitem amount
000 A0 900 1000
001 A0 915 -1000
001 A1 9151500

 result: we have 1000 on opening balance (900) and 500 on periodic changes (915) with an inception-to-date balance loaded of 1500 on A1.

entry.title: Re: Multiply language character display using same font at Adobe form
entry.content:
A few points need to be checked:

1, The GUI Logon language matters even for the Adobe forms! Don't expect 100% perfect for all texts especially those maintained in different languages while logon GUI with EN.

2, The source of those special texts matters. It may be fine to pass through driver programs that may not especially for the SO10 text objects, check the SPRAS!

3, Arial is the best default font if one form needs adapting for different languages. Use a dynamic font with Javascript if any.

entry.title: Document splitting at profit center level
entry.content:
We are able to see this node "Document Splitting Characteristic for General Ledgers " in CBC but the tick box for Zero balance is still greyed out.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Document splitting at profit center level
entry.content:
SAP Help Portal: Document Splitting Characteristics

Changes to the Profit Center characteristic are possible only if no journal entries exist in the production system.
Here, you can only select the Mandatory check box. However, the Zero Balance check box will automatically be selected when you save your changes.

So it's standard system behavior.

entry.title: Re: PCOE ::: how to set business hours?
entry.content: Dear Customer, Could you please reach out to the SV-BO-REQ-PCOE team for the answer your question? Thank you. Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: how to manage user authority?
entry.content: user authority

entry.title: Re: General Display Name Opt out in Second Half of 2024
entry.content:
@Carlo1_1990 Thank you for feedback. If customer would not like to opt-in. They can choose opt-out after the running of migration job. You can opt it out in Company system and logo setting->Enable Adoption of General Display Name. 

Enable general display will follow the current format if it was configured previously on employee profile name format.

entry.title: Re: Vector Engines Overview
entry.content:
Please include such articles in the Blog post section .
As otherwise this feeds take part into unanswered question reporting

entry.title: Re: how to inject the clientId/secret to OAuth2UserTokenE...
entry.content: Hi, your MTA's look correct to me (even though it would be simpler to share a single xsuaa instance between both the srv and approuter), but it's unclear to me what the current situation is. First we need to ensure that the destination is created correctly, not like in your first screenshot. Is that the case? And that you don't have other destinations with this name from previous tests. Then it's about ensuring the approuter uses this destination with token forwarding, so the Destination Service can do the token exchange.

entry.title: Re: non operating expense or income
entry.content: Non-operting expense and income accounts are also used for intercompany cost allocations with subsequent intercompany billing. Further infos about this process can be found here https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/intercompany-cost-allocations-in-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13442721

entry.title: Send notifications in ATC only in error cases
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to use ATC to run unit tests in packages automatically and periodically.

Furthermore I would like to send a notification, e.g. via mail or SBWP, for each run to specific receivers.

So far, I know that this is possible.

Now, I am looking for a way only to send notifications if at least 1 unit tests fails.

Is this possible? Can anyone give me a tip?

BR

entry.title: Re: how to set a constant attribute to strans xml transformation?
entry.content:
I guess you want to obtain this:

 

<issuance xmlns="http://www.w3schools.com" sender="700069297">
</issuance>

 

Your expectation is incorrect, both forms are valid and strictly identical, and will be accepted by all the XML readers I know.

There's no way you can obtain the end tag.

entry.title: Re: Workflow Based Approval of Payment Proposal
entry.content:
Hi,

I did all config. no idea why its not working. please guide if there are any other steps missing.

entry.title: Re: API Error "Error in document: BKPFF $ 0M5R9UL"
entry.content:
Hello,

The errors should be clear enough.

Please solve the errors one by one:

FOT_TDT 008
Time dependent taxes: tax date has to be filled from caller

FOT_TDT 012
For tax code A3, the key date 00/00/0000 does not fall in any validity period

FF825
No tax rate defined for company code 1510 transaction FTXP A3

F5507
G/L account is not defined in chart of accounts YCOA

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: How to transpose rows to columns in abap
entry.content: how convert column to row internal table report in sap abap ?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP - Event Mesh Consumption of Messages Not working
entry.content:
Your listener is only defined in the "this.on('messagePersistence')" function block, so gets deleted the moment the function executed. Try this:

const messaging = await cds.connect.to('messaging')

messaging.on('test/cap/dev/publisher', msg => {
  console.log(msg.data)
})

this.on('messagePersistence', async (req) => {
  await messaging.emit({
    event: 'test/cap/dev/publisher',
    data: `Hello`
  })
  console.log('Messaging Emitted Done')
  return 'OK'
})

entry.title: Re: Bring your own Git for SAP BTP ABAP Environment

thanks for your candid feedback! Yes, there are plans to support also custom on-premise git services, e.g., using SAP Cloud Connector. However, there is no committed roadmap yet for this feature.

entry.title: How to validate at least one digital signature available in PDF file or not
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

I am processing some invoices but before processing I have to validate if at least one digital signature available in the same PDF file or not.

I tried to check some solutions here and there but have not found any without API and ADS.

Is there any solution/option to achieve this without using API and ADS?

 

I would appreciate your answer.

 

Regards,

Maneesha

entry.title: Putaway Strategy Configuration
entry.content:
For a particular Material, we need to assign 5 Storage bins.

When we create a Transfer order after the Good receipt process; the system should automatically suggest the first Bin.

And if the first bin is full, system should automatically suggest the next bin based on the bin capacity. Error should be generated if we try any other bins other than the assigned ones (5 bins).

Is this possible in SAP WM? Or EWM? What all configurations are required for this requirement?

entry.title: Re: Tcode for Clear PSA Fiori Application
entry.content:
Hey EszterN,

Thanks very much for your suggestion.
I was able to find a way to release the stock reference from Warehouse monitor itself.
In the PMR stock list you have the option to Release the Stock reference.

entry.title: External Refurbishment in Asset Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
entry.content:
The refurbishment of repairable spares is important for companies, for which continuity of operation is a critical factor (for example, the process industry, oil producing and processing, steel processing) and for which repairable spares guarantee a high level of system availability. The refurbishment of usually high value faulty repairable spares is of considerable economic importance for these companies and is often a core process in Plant Maintenance. It is often much more cost-effective than a brand-new purchase.

In production plants or other functional location/equipment, high value components are often used (pumps, motors and so on) which are replaced in case of damage by a functional repairable spare and then refurbished using a separate order.

The refurbishment of repairable spares in plant maintenance is integrated with inventory management and materials planning.

Let's see how this requirement can be realized with current version of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition with existing functionalities.

Prerequisites

  • A material master record must be created in the system for the repairable spares.
  • Unique identification is only possible if the individual pieces of material have been serialized.

In current version of S/4HANA Asset management Cloud you can use an existing order type for the refurbishment with an activity type indicating the order used for external refurbishment.

Master Data

Serialized equipment that needs to be refurbished.

Serialized Equipment Master

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The serialized material master with split valuation with the below valuation types -DAMAGED,NEW,ZREFURB(refurbished)

Valuation Types

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Material Master with Split valuation.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Process Flow

Below process flow explains the external refurbishment in brief followed by steps executed in system in detail.

 External Refurbishment Process Flow

 

Steps

Step 1: Create a maintenance order with Maintenance activity type “External refurbishment”.

Maintenance Order

Step 2: Update the valuation and Dismantle Equipment that needs to be refurbished.

Dsimantled Equipment master with updated Valuation

Step 3: Add negative quantity damaged material to operation to bring it to warehouse with movement 262

Maintenance Order with added Component

On goods issue against maintenance order, the damaged material is moved to warehouse with movement type 262

Goods issue with 262 Movement

Stock overview has the material with DAMAGED valuation in unrestricted stock. You can right click and verify the Serial number associated.

Damaged Material in Unrestricted stock

Damaged material updated with stock information

Step 4: Next step is to manually create a subcontracting purchase requisition for refurbished material with Account assignment “F” (Order)and item category “L”(Subcontracting).

Subcontracting Purchase Requisition

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

With the account assignment “F” you can associate the PR with maintenance order

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The subcontracting type selected is” Refurbishment with unchanged material number”, as in this case, we are requisitioning to get the same Damaged material returned after refurbishment in Refurbished state from the Vendor with the same Serial number.

Subcontracting types available

 

 

 

 

Subcon PR with subcontracting type selected

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The component details automatically get updated with the same material number for this rework scenario and the Material Provision indicator “S”(rework material) is updated. Specify the Valuation type of this issuing component as Damaged. 

Purchase Requisition Component

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 5: Convert Purchase Requisition to Purchase order. Make sure to input right serial number.

Subcontracting Purchase Order

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 6: Transfer Posting against Purchase Order with movement type 541 will Move defect stock to Vendor stock

Transfer Postings

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

As below, the damaged material is reflecting under “stock provided to Vendor.”

Damaged material moved to Vendor Stock

Equipment Master updated with stock information

Step 7: Once the material is refurbished, the goods receipt against purchase order will receive the refurbished material into warehouse.

Movement type 101: Refurbished material is received into warehouse.

Movement type 543: Consumption of stock of material provided to Vendor

Goods receipt

Stock updated with refurbished valuation type.

Refurbished material in Unrestricted stock and Damaged material moved out from Vendor Stock

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Serial number verified with updated stock information.

Equipment Master updated with Stock information

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The refurbished material can then be consumed on the maintenance order and manually installed to superior equipment/functional location.

Any external services can be added on the maintenance order with standard lean services functionality.

As for the settlement, in standard refurbishment process the costs are settled to the material. But in SAP S/4HANA cloud, public edition the maintenance orders support settlement either on cost centre or WBS only. So, check with your finance consultant on how the settlement can be worked out in this case.

Conclusion

This should help you build an external refurbishment scenario for repairable spares in Maintenance orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition Asset Management.

Hope this is helpful. Thanks for your time and please share your valuable feedback and inputs in a comment below. Also kindly follow the tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management to watch similar content.

You can also post your questions at All Questions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management | SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Good morning,I have 2 senior leaders are not able to...
entry.content: The links are based in the SuccessFactors Customer Corner and require a customer license associated with the UID of the user trying to access them.

entry.title: Re: Determine class structure / components (local class,...
entry.content: Note that, although it's abnormal, test classes can be defined outside the CCAU include. If you don't want to parse the code yourself, and you are processing only CUSTOM classes, you may try to find the table containing the cross-reference information generated to build the tree of "sub-objects" displayed in ADT or SE80, or read the table TAUNIT_META_DATA which contains the test classes after execution.

entry.content: Informed expert.

entry.title: Re: We have copied new company code in S/4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Please share details about the error ID and text.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: Tcode for Clear PSA Fiori Application

entry.title: Re: Create Role Customer (Fin.Accounting) (FLCU00) in Mass. Is this possible in Cloud?

entry.title: Re: How to use MAN_CALL to send other document than MSDS
entry.content:
Hi Christoph,

I realized the solution recommended by note 1055907 was implemented a few years ago. This was applied to be able to send a different email object and core depending if it is MAN_CALL, SD_CALL or SUB_CALL. The two programs indicated in the note were modified consequently and the solution of cover sheet was deactivated.

Based on the notice, I think the better way to proceed with "CPDC" document with MAN_CALL is to enhance again these programs.

What do you think ?

Thanks

Daman

entry.title: Re: Custom ABAP CDS Views in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Arthur,

yes, you can create analytical query as CDS view entities and consume in SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more general information in the overview blog post: Developing for Analytics with the SAP BTP ABAP Environment or in the Devtoberfest session: Introduction to Embedded Analytics with ABAP Cloud.

Corresponding documentation in ABAP Analytics and hands-on examples/tutorials here:

  • Analytical Data Modeling & Live Data Connection to SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Develop and Consume Queries on SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Develop Queries Based on Booking Supplement and Consume on SAP Analytics Cloud

Best regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: IAS/ SSO login for user with two different SF usernam...
entry.content:
Hi Zeba,

I addition to methods suggested by Diego, I would also consider using different attribute as Subject Name Identifier on IAS. You can change the configuration to use different attribute on IAS for SSO instead of e-mail, but you need to make sure that this attribute is synchronized from SF to IAS with IPS. 

Abdul.

entry.title: Re: Solution Manager 7.2 - System recommendations tile no...
entry.content: Dear Customer, Please try the resolution steps in KBA 2334184 - Component controller 'com.sap.solman.sysrec.Component' could not be found. Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: Strategic Decision Paths for Managing Corporate Carbon Footprint Calculations (SCT, SFM, EHS-EM)
entry.content:
Summary

This article guides businesses through the architectural decision-making process regarding SAP solutions for calculating corporate carbon footprint. It focuses on three specific SAP solutions: SAP Sustainability Control Tower, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP EHS Environment Management, to clarify the best solution paths tailored to different customer business requirements within the broader scope of Carbon Footprint calculations. 

 

Business Requirements

Corporate Carbon Footprint is the total amount of GHG emissions directly or indirectly caused by a company’s activities. Emissions are usually calculated for a specific period, such as a calendar year. 

Regulatory frameworks like CSRD, ESRS, EFRS, SEC are evolving quickly and demanding corporate level reporting. Organizations are required to communicate and demonstrate accountability for their impacts on the environment, economy and people and to create a transparent data foundation for decarbonization of own operations and supply chain. This is done by calculating the Greenhouse Gases: Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions according to GHG-Protocol 

 

Solution Options

SAP offers three primary solution paths for calculating Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) :

  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Ideal for low maturity customers in offering a single solution for entry level CCF calculations and reporting (as an answer for forcing events (regulations) 
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Enabling seamless transition towards a high mature and granular Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) and Product Carbon Footprint (PCF) calculation  
  • SAP EHS-Environment Management: Manage all Greenhouse Gas Emissions inventory to fulfill legal regulatory and permit compliance as well as voluntary and corporate requirements 

 

Characterization of the Solution Options

SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) 

Focus: Assist organizations monitor and steer ESG (Environment, Social and Government) targets and report on sustainability performance while helping to measure and manage ESG transparency across the value chain 

CCF Focus: Form based Scope 1, 2 and 3 emission 

Outcome:  

  • Entry Level capabilities of calculating scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions via SAP’s guided CCF forms 
  •  Embed GHG & CO2e into strategic decision-making by using KPI reporting based on established frameworks and emerging reporting standards 
  • Proactively manage and steer towards sustainability targets 
  • Give business units a dedicated view on their sustainability performance and indication of improvement areas 

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM) 

Focus: Provide transparency of an organizations carbon footprint (CO2e) on product, value chain and corporate level for mandatory or voluntary requirements 

CCF Focus: Advanced capabilities of calculating scope 1,2 and 3 emissions via SAP’s guided CCF forms or/and network-based calculation. Leveraging ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) centric calculation for more automation and granularity. 

Outcome: 

  • Calculate scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions with advanced methodologies to improve accuracy. 
  • Reuse transactional data from SAP S/4HANA to automate and scalele calculation. 
  • Use supplier specific data for advanced accuracy via SDX integration. 
  • Establish a transparent and auditable process for calculating CO2e from data collection to reporting. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EHS Environment Management 

Focus: Manage all GHG emissions inventory to fulfill legal regulatory and permit compliance as well as voluntary and corporate requirements 

CCF Focus: Scope 1 and 2 emissions based on actuals. 

Outcome: 

  • Calculation of greenhouse gas inventories with actual emission amounts 
  • Equations, regulations, and emission factor content built-in for Scope 1 and 2 GHG Protocol emission outputs 
  • Improve accuracy through emission measurements  
  • Reuse EHS data from energy-, waste- and water monitoring systems 
  • Ensure license to operate 
  • Establish a transparent and auditable process for calculating actuals of GHG emissions from data collection to reporting 
  • Detect, communicate, act, and report upon actuals GHG compliance deviations 

 

Criteria to determine the most suitable target solution

Examples of qualifying questions 

  • Is the client planning to move to S/4HANA through a RISE approach? 
  • Is the client able to implement public cloud solutions for carbon footprint calculations? 
  • What is the maturity level and appetite of the client in the context of CCF? 
  • Which GHG protocol scope categories are in focus/most important for the client? 
  • What are GHG levels of breakdown? 
  • What are the client’s current and future sources of data for Scope 1,2,3 emission calculations? 
  • What is the client’s business, manufacturer vs service provider? 
  • Based on the client’s business model and operations, is there value in calculating carbon footprint at a product level? 
  • Does the client currently run SAP EHS for any process supporting sustainability? 

 

Evaluation Spreadsheet

 

Criteria/Options 

SCT 

SFM 

EHS-EM 

Migration to S/4HANA not through RISE 

no 

no 

yes 

CCF Maturity Level 

low 

medium 

high 

GHG Protocol Scope Categories? 

Carbon Footprint (CO2e) Reporting 

 

Carbon Footprint (CO2e) Management 

 

All environmental aspects including air pollutants, water pollutants, GHG emission, and waste.  

  1. GHG emissions inventory management 
    Air emissions management 
  1. Waste management 
  1. Water & wastewater management 
  1. Carbon Footprint (CO2e) 

 

GHG Levels of Breakdown 

Only Carbon Dioxide equivalent (CO2e) 

 

Only Carbon Dioxide equivalent (CO2e) 

 

Manage each individual GHG emissions inventory (e.g. CO2, MH4, N2O, HFCs, NMVOCs, PFCs, SF6)  

Able to convert all GHG emission amounts into Carbon Dioxide equivalent (CO2e) values 

 

Customer Business/Industry 

Retail/High Tech/Entertainment 

Utilities/Food and Beverage 

Oil&Gas/Transportation 

Data sources for Scope 1,2,3 emissions? 

Aggregated Averages 

Form Based 

ERP Data/Supplier Specific Data 

Calculating carbon footprint at product level? 

yes 

yes 

yes 

EHS already available? 

yes 

yes 

yes 

 

Decision Tree

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, selecting the appropriate SAP solution on corporate carbon footprint calculations depends on your organization's specific requirements. 

 Recommendations Based on Customer Focus/Scope 

  • Operational Level/All environmental aspects including air pollutants, water pollutants, GHG emission, and waste : EHS – EM is the solution recommended if customer needs/wants to manage and monitor each individual GHG emissions inventory (e.g. CO2, MH4, N2O, HFCs, NMVOCs, PFCs, SF6)  and also be able to convert all GHG emission amounts into Carbon Dioxide equivalent (CO2e) values.
  • Tactical & Strategic Level/Focus on calculating an organization’s carbon footprint on a Corporate and Product Level: SAP SFM is the solution recommended if customer needs/ wants to measure their product and corporate carbon footprint. 
  • Reporting & Strategic Level/ Entry level calculations and reporting in one single solution: SAP SCT is the solution recommended. 

 
Please note that these are complementary solutions. The most common use case is: 

  • SAP EHS-EM handless calculations for process emissions and fugitive emissions. Then converts into CO2e and hands over to SFM to be fed into Manual Emissions, and then to be allocated on corporate and a product footprint level.

entry.title: Re: Leveraging Generative AI Advancements for Linked Business Data
entry.content: @ME42 : The KG blog post was published here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/knowledge-graphs-for-llm-grounding-and-avoiding-hallucination/ba-p/13779734

entry.title: Re: Get a Product's parts price history from SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

With current function, it's possible to maintain periodical prices for materials.

However, it's not possible to retain each change of price during the period.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.content:

entry.title: This document cannot be deleted Message no. V1006
entry.content:
HI Expert

Fire Urgent Please

I canceled my Sale Invoice and PGI, and then I came back with my Sales OrderVA02 but the system does not allow me to delete the Sales Order and line item on the Sales Order. The system is response currently restricting the deletion of the line item in the Sales Order

When I am deleting the Sales Order or Line item in sale order its giving a message like this "

1st Sales Order Deleting Message :This document cannot be deleted Message no. V1006

2nd Sales Order Line Item Deleting Message: For reason of cost management, Item 000010 cannot be Deleted Message no. V1128

 Maintained MRP1 MRP Type ND/PD Both and MRP3, Strategy Group 10/20
Sales Order Type Domestic Dealer Sales

Can anyone explain me about this and can give me solution how to delete this Sales Order or Line item please??

 

 

Thanks 
M.G Rabbani

entry.title: Re: Using BTP Authentication and Destinations with SAP Build Apps (formerly AppGyver) *and deploy it
entry.content:
@MarcHuber 

@Murali_Shanmugham 

@Murali_Shanmu 

Hi Marc and SAP Team,

 In BTP subaccount where the BuildApps service is subscribed, we have configured BTP IAS tenant as our IDP proxy and it forwards the user authentication to Microsoft Entra IDP.

Our developer has chosen the BTP authentication and selected the backend destination (HTTP destination with principal propagation enabled to backend SAP Gateway / ECC system with relevant rules in the connected cloud connector).

When the developer tries to launch the app, it takes to the BTP login page and expecting to authenticate against the "platform IDP" and not to the "Application IDP" configured in the Trust configuration section of Security section at the BTP sub account level.

Because of this principal propagation to backend is failing. How can we configure the build app to authentication against the "Application IDP" so it will redirect the authentication to Microsoft Entra IDP instead of looking at the BTP IDP local users.

 

Regards,

Nalla

entry.title: Re: BPA - Process Automation for Project Team Assignments
entry.content:
Hi Hanu,

It is quite simple:

1. Get running instance of the SAP HANA Cloud

2. Get SAP Business Application Studio

3. In the SAP Business Application Studio start a Full Stack Cloud Application project in a workspace with SAP HANA Calculation View Editor and SAP HANA Tools added to the workspace

4. Connect your development environment to your Cloud Foundry runtime environment

5. Connect your development environment to your SAP HANA Cloud instance

6. Define entities of your database in the CDS file in the db folder of your project

7. Define your service in the CDS file in the srv folder of your project

8. build and deploy CDS

9. Build and deploy the service

It may look a lot of steps and complicated task, but in fact it is simple step by step task anybody can do. I can guide you through the entire process, but first you need to secure access to:

- SAP HANA Cloud - capacity depends on your planned data volume

- SAP Business Application Studio - as many licenses as many OData interface developers you plan to employ - probably just one, for you 🙂

- SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Runtime - size depends on volume of data to be processed by the OData interface, but I would suggest 2GB.

We can go forward when you have access to all three above services.

Best regards

Jakub

entry.title: Re: non operating expense or income
entry.content: i think the answer you are looking for is how to classify it with these 4 options mentioned. It can be D or B if your finance team requires non operating revenue or expense to be a part of profitability analysis

entry.title: Re: Unable to (re)create RFC destinations in solman_setup

entry.title: Re: Configuration Tab missing in PDS screen (/n/SAPAPO/CU...
entry.content: Hi Smax, thanks for your reply. The information provided in the response was helpful. Thanks again

entry.title: Re: Manage Schedule [F3326] - Share schedule - Search hel...
entry.content:
Hi Radek,

is this public cloud or private cloud (resp. on premise)?

Please note that in private cloud there is another prerequisite note mentioned in the product assistance:

  • Planners with whom you want to share a schedule must have assigned at least one work center to themselves.

    Only if this prerequisite is fulfilled will the planner's name be available in the Share Schedule dialog.

Note

If all these prerequisites are fulfilled but you still can't add another planner or reviewer in the Share Schedule dialog, contact your key user or administrator. App users with whom you want to share a schedule must be available as business users.

Please find the help links for private cloud:

  • Sharing a Schedule
  • When to setup business users

 

Hope this helps.

Kind Regards,
Clemens

entry.title: Re: Unable to (re)create RFC destinations in solman_setup
entry.content:
Hello Vera,

thanks a lot. No, the user is not locked, that was the very first thing I checked. As I wrote, I can login to the 
system with this user in SAP GUI without any issues.
I am rather wondering why the RFC authorization test is popping up a window. There is probably some other
security parameter that tightens up security way too much 😕

entry.title: Re: Unable to (re)create RFC destinations in solman_setup
entry.content: The error doesn't talk about the DDIC user. It talks about RFC user, mostly the READ. Did you check READ RFC user? Which RFC did you test and got the login pop up screen?

entry.title: Changes in Workflow is not reflecting in quality system
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We had a custom workflow of 3 levels. Now we have added one level before the final level making it as 4 levels. In our development system it is working fine for new and old reports. The workflow graphical log is automatically getting adjusted and the old approvals are now following as per the new structure.

But when we moved it to Quality system, the new reports are working as per the 4 levels but the old ones which were there in the approval states are following the old 3 level structure. 

I have tried SWU_OBUF and PPWFBUF in the quality system but its still not working.

Kindly help.

Thanks and regards,

Priyanka.

entry.title: Re: Refused to display in a frame because it set 'X-Frame-Options' to 'deny'.
entry.content:
Hi, we are facing the same issue in SAP Build Process Automation. It happens when we want to add a new dependency in a project and as well when we want to embed a task in the task center or better said just open it there. One year since somebody asked and still no solution?

It happens in Chrome and Firefox. Tested both. X-Frame-Options are set to deny.

entry.title: Re: HANA log segments and its states
entry.content:
Hello All,

Is there anyway to find historic log segment status?. I mean, I need to get the log segment status 2 week before.

entry.title: SPM Embedded Analytics Insights with SAC Publications
entry.content:
As organizations continue to leverage data to drive business decisions, the ability to efficiently share insights and reports becomes increasingly crucial. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) offers a powerful feature known as Publications, which allows users to automate the distribution of stories and dashboards to a broad audience. In this blog, we’ll dive into the SAC Publication feature, explore key areas of scheduling, and highlight its pivotal role in streamlining operations.

What is SAC Publication?

The SAC Publication feature enables users to schedule and distribute stories and dashboards to designated recipients automatically. Whether you need to share daily sales reports, monthly performance metrics, or ad-hoc analyses, SAC Publications ensures that the right people receive the right information at the right time.

The Role of Publications in Embedded Analytics (EA)

The Publication feature plays a pivotal role within Embedded Analytics (EA), particularly for Compensation Administrators. It allows them to schedule the generation and delivery of both Published Reports and Personalized Reports to end users.

  • Published Reports: These are standardized reports generated by EA that provide consistent insights across the organization.
  • Personalized Reports: These reports are customized to cater to individual preferences, needs, or specific requirements of users.

The system enables Compensation Administrators to automate the report generation process, ensuring timely and tailored delivery of crucial information directly to users' inboxes. This capability streamlines operations, granting administrators precise control over report distribution and enhancing the overall efficiency of the reporting process.

Key Features of SAC Publication

  1. Automated Scheduling:
    • Time-Saving Automation: Instead of manually exporting and sending reports, SAC allows you to automate this process. Reports can be scheduled to run at specific times, ensuring timely delivery.
    • Frequency Options: Users can choose from a variety of scheduling options, such as daily, weekly, monthly, or even custom intervals, depending on the needs of the organization.
  2. Format Flexibility:
    • Multiple Formats: SAC supports various output formats, including PDF, Microsoft Excel, and PowerPoint. This flexibility allows users to tailor the report format to the preferences of the recipients.
    • Personalized Content: SAC Publications can be customized to include specific filters or views, ensuring that each recipient receives relevant data.
  3. Secure Distribution:
    • Access Control: Publications are distributed securely, respecting the data access rights and permissions of each recipient. This ensures that sensitive information is only shared with authorized users.
    • Recipient Management: You can easily manage and update the list of recipients, including individuals and groups, within SAC.
  4. Error Handling and Notifications:
    • Error Alerts: If a scheduled publication encounters issues, SAC can notify administrators or designated users, allowing for quick resolution.
    • Success Notifications: Recipients can also receive notifications when a new report is available, ensuring they are aware of the latest insights.

Key Areas of Scheduling in SAC

Scheduling plays a critical role in the effective use of SAC Publications. Here are some key areas to consider:

  1. Defining the Schedule:
    • Timing: Consider the optimal time for report distribution. For example, scheduling a report to be sent at the start of the business day ensures that decision-makers have the latest data available when they begin work.
    • Frequency: Determine how often the data needs to be refreshed and reported. High-frequency scheduling (e.g., daily) may be necessary for dynamic, fast-paced environments, while less frequent updates may suffice for static or long-term analyses.
  2. Selecting the Right Audience:
    • Targeted Distribution: Identify the key stakeholders who need access to the reports. By segmenting your audience, you can ensure that each group receives information that is most relevant to their roles.
    • Role-Based Access: Use SAC’s role-based access controls to ensure that only authorized users receive sensitive data, maintaining compliance with data governance policies.
  3. Managing Report Formats:
    • Standardization: Standardizing the report format across the organization can help in maintaining consistency and ease of interpretation. Decide on the most suitable format (PDF, Excel, etc.) based on the needs of your audience.
    • Customization: While standardization is important, consider offering customized views or filtered data for specific recipients, especially if they require a focused analysis.
  4. Monitoring and Optimization:
    • Reviewing Schedules: Regularly review and adjust publication schedules to align with changing business needs or recipient feedback.
    • Performance Monitoring: Monitor the performance of scheduled publications to ensure they are delivered without delays or errors. SAC’s built-in monitoring tools can help identify bottlenecks or issues.

Step-by-Step Guide: How to Schedule a Publication in SAC

Automating the report generation process involves scheduling jobs to run at predefined intervals, ensuring regular and systematic generation of reports without manual intervention. Here’s how you can schedule a publication:

  1. Login to Embedded Analytics.
  2. Navigate to the Public folder.
  3. Select a Story and open it.
  4. Click on File -> Schedule Publication.
     
     

    • The Schedule Publication window will open. Enter the details Event Name, Start Date, Destination, Email Subject, Internal Users and Select File Type and click Create.

 

    • Upon successful creation, navigate to Calendar to view the scheduled publication.

    • Open the publication to review details and settings.
  1. Verify the Publication:
    • Once the publication is successful, verify that the generated PDF report is delivered to the user’s inbox.

    • Open the user folder and check the generated report to ensure accuracy and completeness.

Best Practices for SAC Publications

  • Test Before Deploying: Always test your publication settings with a small group before rolling them out organization-wide. This helps identify any potential issues with format, content, or timing.
  • Document Your Schedule: Maintain documentation of your publication schedules and configurations. This will be useful for troubleshooting and ensuring continuity in case of staff changes.
  • Regularly Update Recipients: Keep your recipient lists up-to-date to ensure that reports are sent to the correct individuals. This is especially important in large organizations with frequent personnel changes.

Conclusion

The SAC Publication feature is a powerful tool for automating the distribution of reports and dashboards, saving time and ensuring consistency across the organization. By understanding the key areas of scheduling and following best practices, you can maximize the efficiency and effectiveness of your reporting processes in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Explore SAC Publications today, and take your reporting strategy to the next level!

This blog provides a comprehensive overview of the SAC Publication feature, emphasizing its importance in Embedded Analytics and offering actionable insights for effective scheduling and automation.

entry.title: Re: Loading inception-to-date balances
entry.content:
Hi Florian, thanks a lot for your reply.

Assuming the closing currency conversion rate of the PY 1.5 and the average currency conversion rate of the current year as 1.7, the approach suggested will generate the following result:

period doc typesubitem amount loc curramount Group Curr
000 A0 900 10001500
001 A0 915 -1000-1700
001 A1 91515002550

In group currency the document type A0 will be different from 0

entry.title: Completing the Digital Blueprint - with the System Scan
entry.content:
The SAP Business Transformation Center – Digital Blueprint provides data insights and supports scoping activities as part of a transformation process.

The Digital Blueprint consists of two elements:

  • an Analysis File based on the SAP Readiness Check Capabilities
  • a System Scan created via direct access to the source system.

 

Why

What is the difference, and why do we need those two approaches?

The SAP Readiness Check (UDP version) provides data insights related to a specific set of standard transformation objects, and the data usage in the organizational structure.  These transformation objects are the basis for scoping decisions (“scopable objects”). However, there are more tables in a system than those covered by the Readiness Check.

 

These tables are covered by the System Scan. It provides a list of all tables that are not covered in the SAP delivered Transformation Object content, which can be migrated from SAP ERP to S/4HANA:

  • Customer-specific tables (Z* and Y*)
  • Third Party tables (e.g., SAP Add-Ons)
  • Dependent standard tables that are required independently of scoping decisions (for example change documents)

It provides information regarding the number of entries counted for each of the tables, to support the users’ scoping decision per table.

Wouldn’t it be possible to implement the UDP RC part into the System Scan?

💡 It would – technically – be an option to derive the information related to the transformation objects within the System Scan, too. However, this would limit the flexibility you have now with the offline approach in the Readiness Check, by manually uploading a file. Remember: With this approach, you can start scoping activities, e.g. in first workshops, without having the hurdle of setting up the system connectivity. 💡

 Note: It is possible to create a Digital Blueprint based on an Analysis File, without assigning a System Scan. However, to be able to confirm the Digital Blueprint and proceed to the Modeling phase, a System Scan is mandatory.

What

The System Scan functionality includes two applications,

  • Manage System Scans, where you can create, execute, and monitor the status of a System Scan, and assign it to a Digital Blueprint
  • Select Scanned Tables to perform and document scoping decisions per table.

How

Note: Before working with the System Scan Apps, you need to set-up the connectivity for the source system (see below, section prerequisites). But for now, let’s start with creating a system scan by accessing the related tile Manage System Scans on SAP Cloud ALM Launchpad:

 

 

In the app, simply press ‘Create new Scan’…

 

…fill out the required fields, and press create:

 

After creating a scan, some initial checks are performed, such as

  • if the connection to the source system maintained correctly (see section ‘Prerequisites’ below)
  • if the required minimum release is met,
  • and if all prerequisite ABAP notes are implemented.

 Now the scan can be started (button top right corner); it will take a few minutes. By refreshing the screen, the list in section Scanned Tables will grow gradually; the Activity Log shows the single processing steps:

 

 

When finished, the result is displayed in section Scanned Tables. In our example, we see standard tables which are not included by the Readiness Check (because they are mandatory for the migration), as well as a long list of custom tables (Z*). As mentioned above, it would also be the spot where add-on tables and tables in partner name space would appear.

 

 

Currently, the result only contains the tables that are relevant for scoping decisions. Tables that are ‘not scopable’ are so far only visible in the Modeling App (in the Transformation Model and Transformation Model Version). However, there will be an update soon to also show auto-scoped tables that will be automatically put into scope (baseline tables) and transferred as part of the migration. This ensures consistency between Scoping and Modeling Apps, and it will help users to gain full transparency on the scope of migration.

Now you can assign this System Scan to your Digital Blueprint.

 

 

Next step is covered by the Select Scanned Tables app. It can be reached either via a tile on the Transition tab on the main page, or via ‘Related Apps’.

 

Same as in the Select Company Codes app, scoping decisions are supported by providing hints and information on the (total and relevant) data count. In this example, tables containing data have been de-scoped:

 

 

As this will be a conscious decision, you might want to document it in the detail screen for the table by using the Comment function.

 

 

Now you are basically done with the scoping of scanned tables, and you might want to set the status to ‘confirmed’ (mass edit or detail) to make it visible. This is not a mandatory step; however, you will receive a reminder (warning) in the Digital Blueprint.

 

 

With completing the scoping of Scanned Tables, the second element of the Digital Blueprint is covered. What is still missing, is the selection of Transformation Objects (TOs).

 

The current scope of the lean Selective Data Transformation covers the scoping based on Company Code level. This means, the set of transformation objects is given. This means you cannot de-scope TOs that have relevant data in the source system. However, there might be TOs for which no relevant data exist, i.e. it is not used in any of the company codes that are in scope. In this case, it is recommended to de-scope the ‘empty’ tables (tables without relevant data), as in this example:

 

 

As you can see, all TOs with a relevant data scope of zero are set out of scope here; for Material Ledger Cost Estimation data do exist – but not for the scoped company codes (0001, 1000 in this example). This can be verified in the detail screen for this object:

 

You might ask why you would do that, if it doesn’t reduce the data footprint, which is true. The reason is that by excluding these TOs, the transformation steps related to those tables are not required - which means this saves effort later in the preparation of the source and target system (see Manage Cycles app, step Preparation).

 

Now you are done with the scoping activities, and you can review the result in the Digital Blueprint Overview app:

 

Prerequisites

The System Scan is the first increment that provides – and requires - direct integration of SAP Business Transformation Center and the SAP ERP source system. This means, a connection from the SAP Cloud ALM tenant to the ERP source system needs to be established:

  • A Cloud Connector must be configured
  • The connection to the source system must be maintained in the Landscape Management Service

For a detailed description on how to set up the connectivity, please refer to the related section in Setup and Administration Guide in SAP Business Transformation Center online help: Setup and Administration Guide

entry.title: Consume SAP Document Management using @cap-js/sdm plugin
entry.content:
In this blog post I am going to write how can you store the attachments(documents) in to SAP Document management repository using SAP Document Management CAP Plugin.

This plugin provides an UI within it which helps the user to attach ' n' number of attachments and save all these attachments in a single click of Save in the SAP Document management repository using CAP application on Business technology platform. The node package that provides the functionality for Document Management is @cap-js/sdm plugin. The source code of this plugin can be found in the repository. https://github.com/cap-js/sdm

The SDM CAP plugin provides out-of-the-box asset storage and handling. To use it, all we need to do is to extend the domain model by using the predefined aspect called Attachments, that is delivered as part of @cap-js/sdm plugin.

Preview: The SDM CAP plugin also add the facets required to display the attachment section on the Object page. This attachment sections uses the control sap.m.upload.UploadSetwithTable.

This plugin enables cmis operations like create Document,Rename,getContentStream and delete document.

User provides the repository Id where the attachments should get stored in package.json under cds.env.requires as below 

"sdm": {
   "settings": {
   "repositoryId": "<repository-Id>"
   }
}

  Follow the below steps to store your documents in SAP Document management repository with an inbuilt UI without the users developing their own UI and just extending their CAP entity with attachments aspect.

1. Users who can want to start fresh with  CAP and want to try out a basic CAP application with SAP Document Management CAP Plugin

Consider a simple bookshop application and users of this application wants to store the cover page, book invoice, softcopy of book etc in to SAP Document Repository then just start creating a book shop CAP application by following the below steps.

 

Step 1: Initialise the CAP Project

Initialise the cap project using the command cds init in the root directory of the project folder.

Execute the command npm install to install the dependencies.

You can now start the cap server using the command cds watch. This will start the cap server on http://localhost:4004.

The service would now be empty without any entities defined.

 

Step 2: Adding Sample CDS Model

To add the sample cds model, execute the command cds add tiny-sample in the terminal.

This will add the entity definition in the db folder and add some sample data in the data folder.

 

namespace my.bookshop;

entity Books {
  key ID : Integer;
  title  : String;
  stock  : Integer;
}
In the srv folder, the book entity is exposed. I have modified the generated code to remove the @readonly and add enabled draft handling.
 
using my.bookshop as my from '../db/data-model';

service CatalogService {
    entity Books as projection on my.Books;
    annotate Books with @odata.draft.enabled;
}

Execute the command cds watch to run the service.

 

 

Step 3: Add the UI5 Application

Open the Fiori Application generator from the menu view -> command palette -> Fiori: Open Application Generator.

In the wizard, select the list report template.

 

 

Map the data source to the local cap project and select the catalog service.

 

Select the books entity.

 

 

Enter the project details and click on finish. This will add the ui5 application in the app folder.

 

 

In the generated project, update the path of the service in the manifest.json. 

 

 

Note:To be able to use the Fiori uploadTable feature, you must ensure 1.121.0/ 1.122.0/ ^1.125.0 SAPUI5 version is updated in the application's index.html

Execute the command cds watch to run the service. Now you should be able to see the ui5 application served.

 

 

The list report page and object page of the application as of now should be as below.

 

 

 

Follow the below blog to set up the approuter which redirects the user via xsuaa fetching the user info and generating token enabling authentication and authorization which is required when you want to test locally

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/test-your-cap-nodejs-applications-with-authentication-locally/ba-p/13417243

If you want to run your application in BTP then just add auth as "jwt" in package.json and deploy the app.

Once the above steps are done jump to point 2 to see how to add SAP Document Management plugin to your bookshop application.

 

2.  If you have your own CAP application deployed on Business Technology platform and just want to use SDM CAP plugin to enable users to store the documents in to SAP Document management Repository.

 

Add the SAP Document Management Plugin.

To add the SAP Document Management plugin, execute the following command from the terminal.

 

npm add @cap-js/sdm
 

In the db folder add a new schema file, let's name it attachment.cds and the following to extend the Book entity with the composition to attachment aspect from the attachment plugin.

Here you should add extend for your entity as the below example is for bookshop one.

 

using {my.bookshop as my} from './data-model';
using {Attachments} from '@cap-js/sdm';

extend my.Books with {
    attachments : Composition of many Attachments;
}
 

And that's it!!. Now you can deploy the application to Business technology platform and store your attachments in to SAP Document Management Repository.

 

Features supported

  • Create 'n' attachments with single click of Save Entity.
  • Delete 'n' added attachments
  • Delete Entity which deletes all the linked attachments within entity
  • Rename a document name
  • Read the attachment(download/preview the attachment based on browser support).

 

Hope this blog helps you in storing your documents to SAP Document Management Repository from CAP Application.

entry.title: Groovy Solution for Commerce Thread Dumps
entry.content:
Groovy Solution for Commerce Thread Dumps

Introduction

Analyse thread dumps sometimes can be difficult, you have to take the thread dump, save on you machine, upload it somewhere to analyse the file and then search for the docs which apply for your case.
Working with SAP Commerce, some times we have to analyse thread dumps to debug issues, find performance-related problems and so on. On Cloud you can rely on Monitoring Tools to find such information, but on local it can be tricky, you are by yourself, so, wouldn't be nice to have something to make it a little easier?
If so, you will appreciate the convenience of this Groovy script, which is engineered specifically for the Hybris Administration Console (HAC) in SAP Commerce. Instead of generating large, complicated thread dumps, this script provides an examination of active threads for quick troubleshooting and diagnostics.

Fingertip Functionality

  1. Procures all active thread IDs and their detailed information.
  2. Compiles an array of threads grouped according to their state for easy analysis.
  3. Gives a rundown of each thread's information including ID, name, and state.
  4. Scans known issues against a hardcoded list of solutions for quick resolution.
  5. Offers a related solution link or commentary for the recognized issues.

How can you utilize the script?

Using the script is a breeze thanks to HAC's script console. Simply run the script in the console and the output can be conveniently viewed directly in the console's "Output" tab.

Also, it runs using multithreading, so the pattern-matching is quick.

You can add more definitions to match the threads and add commentaries in case you are seaching for something specific.

Deciphering the Results

The output provided can be a key instrument for debugging and optimizing performance. The output for each thread includes the thread name, its state and the stack trace. Furthermore, you can cross-reference recognized issues with the solutions provided in the form of a link or comments. This can greatly assist in understanding and resolving the situation promptly.

Quick Heads-Up

Remember that the script only returns the threads for the machine it's currently running on. If you're running the script on SAP Commerce Cloud, it will return the threads from the service replica where it was initiated.

Disclaimer

While the script is intended for convenience, it's important to employ it carefully. The process of acquiring and examining thread data can potentially impose on system performance. Always ensure your system's capacity and health before running thread analysis.

The Script

import java.lang.management.ManagementFactory
import java.lang.management.ThreadInfo
import java.lang.management.ThreadMXBean
import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference

ThreadMXBean threadMXBean = ManagementFactory.getThreadMXBean()

long[] threadIds = threadMXBean.getAllThreadIds()
ThreadInfo[] threadInfos = threadMXBean.getThreadInfo(threadIds, Integer.MAX_VALUE)

def threadsByState = threadInfos.findAll { it != null }.groupBy { it.threadState }

def threadStatesOrder = [
    Thread.State.RUNNABLE,
    Thread.State.BLOCKED,
    Thread.State.WAITING,
    Thread.State.TIMED_WAITING,
    Thread.State.NEW,
    Thread.State.TERMINATED
]
def solutions = [
    
      [
        name: "hybrisHTTP",
        state: Thread.State.BLOCKED,
        classMethod: "org.springframework.web.servlet.mvc.method.annotation.RequestMappingHandlerAdapter.handleInternal",
        link: "Try to check https://me.sap.com/notes/3153768"
    ],
      [
        name: "hybrisHTTP",
        state: Thread.State.WAITING,
        classMethod: "de.hybris.platform.tx.DefaultAfterSaveListenerRegistry.enqueueChangesForAsyncNotification",
        link: "Try to check https://me.sap.com/notes/3118007 and https://me.sap.com/notes/3158467" 
    ],
      [
        name: "hybrisHTTP",
        state: Thread.State.BLOCKED,
        classMethod: "org.apache.commons.lang.builder.HashCodeBuilder.reflectionHashCode",
        link: "Try to check https://me.sap.com/notes/2589737"
    ],
      [
        name: "hybrisHTTP",
        state: Thread.State.RUNNABLE,
        classMethod: "java.lang.Thread.run",
        link: "Just a Normal SAP Commerce thread"
    ],
  [
        name: "hybrisHTTP",
        state: Thread.State.RUNNABLE,
        classMethod: "java.lang.Thread.run",
        link: "Just a Normal SAP Commerce thread"
    ],
    [
        name: "Thread",
        state: Thread.State.TIMED_WAITING,
        classMethod: "de.hybris.platform.core.threadregistry.RegistrableThread.run",
        link: "Threads which contains only Thread-<number> on name and are deployed by threadregistry.RegistrableThread.run, are background threads that can include even this script"
    ] //Add more matchings here
]


threadStatesOrder.parallelStream().forEach(state -> {
    if (threadsByState[state]) {
        System.out.println("Threads in state: " + state);
        threadsByState[state].parallelStream().forEach(threadInfo -> {
        System.out.println("Thread name: " + threadInfo.getThreadName());
        System.out.println("Thread state: " + threadInfo.getThreadState());
        System.out.println("Thread ID: " + threadInfo.getThreadId());

        AtomicReference<String> solutionLink = new AtomicReference<>();
        
        Arrays.stream(threadInfo.getStackTrace()).parallel().forEach(stackTraceElement -> {
            solutions.parallelStream().forEach(solution -> {
                if (threadInfo.getThreadName().contains(solution.name) &&
                    threadInfo.getThreadState() == solution.state &&
                    (stackTraceElement.className + "." + stackTraceElement.methodName).contains(solution.classMethod)) {
                        solutionLink.set(solution.link);
                    }
                });
            });

        String link = solutionLink.get();

        if (link != null) {
            System.out.println("Solution or comments: " + link);
        }

        System.out.println("Stack trace:");
        Arrays.stream(threadInfo.getStackTrace()).parallel().forEach(stackTraceElement -> {
            System.out.println("\t" + stackTraceElement);
        });
        
        System.out.println("-".repeat(80));

        });
    }
});

entry.title: Re: Empowering Public Sector Users of SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Business AI

entry.title: Re: PCOE ::: how to set business hours?

entry.title: Re: while production order reversal 261 movement value is not matching with 262 movement
entry.content: what is the price control of the materials in 261 and 262 ? if it is S, it can be different based on valuation price at the time of issue and cancellation. The difference should hit PRD key

entry.title: Re: When deploying an app in Build Apps with Cloud Foundr...
entry.content:
Hi Amy,

Actually applications build in the SAP Build Apps use Cloud Foundry environment for deployment, but not the one you subscribe as a separate service under tha name of SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Runtime. These apps are deployed in the Cloud Foundry runtime included in the SAP Build Apps subscription - this is the reason why you don't see these applications in your CF space.

SAP Build Apps is licensed as the base package, advanced users, standard users and capacity units.

When you subscribe to the base package, you get licensed for 25 advanced users, who are developers, admins and ordinary users AND for the capacity units. The capacity units are in fact translating to the network traffic, storage and projects. When you deploy your app, it consumes the capacity units of your base package of SAP Build Apps. When your apps grow, you have to add the capacity units to your SAP Build Apps subscription.

In the BTP Cockpit you see your applications as service instances - the ones with strange names, like buildapps-12345-html and buildapps-12345-dest.

Answering your questions:

1 - your apps consume CF runtime, but not the one you can subscribe to as SAP BTP CF Runtime - you don't need to subscribe SAP BTP CF Runtme to use SAP Build Apps.

2 - there is no cost associated with the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Runtime service, but there is a cost of the SAP Build Apps capacity units - if you build more projects or big applications, consuming a lot of storage, network traffic or runtime space.

entry.title: VBOF sap rebates agreement
entry.content:
Hi expert

I have a main list of rebates agreement   

I would like to know if there is any tips to identify the agreements  without VBOF run.

entry.title: Re: EWM How to delete incorrectly created Party Entitled...
entry.content:
Hi 

We can not delete Party to entitle dispose. How every if it has been created  incorrectly so We can not remove the plant and block it in BP transaction and remove from warehouse and Map Storage Locations from ERP System to EWM

We will create Correct party to entitle dispose and extend product master data to it and  Also it is required to assign then assign it to warehouse  

 

Also Assign 

ERP Integration > General Settings > Goods Movements > Map Storage Locations from ERP System to EWM

 

I hope this will help you 

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: EWM How to delete incorrectly created Party Entitled...
entry.content: HI,The error is the link of the Party to entitle dispose and the product

entry.title: Re: CSV to XML Converter with(multiple header and Footer)...
entry.content: its working now thanks but can i can skip header and footer from records

entry.title: Re: EWM How to delete incorrectly created Party Entitled...
entry.content: HI, The error is the link of the Party to entitle dispose and the product. we have 2 party's linked and I want to remove 1 of them.

entry.title: Re: EWM How to delete incorrectly created Party Entitled...
entry.content:
Hi 

We can not delete Party to entitle dispose. How every if it has been created  incorrectly so We can not remove the plant and block it in BP transaction and remove from warehouse and Map Storage Locations from ERP System to EWM

We will create Correct party to entitle dispose and extend product master data to it and  Also it is required to assign then assign it to warehouse  

 

Also Assign 

ERP Integration > General Settings > Goods Movements > Map Storage Locations from ERP System to EWM

 

I hope this will help you 

 

I am unable to understand why it is giving error in PO , can you tell me what error message it is coming 

 

Regards

Rashid Khan
